name: Vasterbotten County (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the historic Vasterbotten arms
silver detailing in the reindeer charge

description: A square blue flag charged centrally with the silver running reindeer of the Vasterbotten provincial arms, with gold antlers and hooves.
history: Vasterbotten County's arms were granted on 18 January 1949, drawing on the older arms of the historic province of Lapland; the flag is the heraldic banner of those arms.

features.symbolism: a silver reindeer in full course on the blue field, with gold antlers and hooves and a red tongue, referring to the reindeer-herding traditions of the historic Lapland that included parts of present-day Vasterbotten.
